telephony_systems.name: Twilio
telephony_systems.authentication_notes: Uses HTTP Basic Authentication. The 'username' is your Twilio Account SID and the 'password' is your Auth Token. Credentials should be sent in the 'Authorization' header as a Base64-encoded string.

telephony_systems.name: Plivo
telephony_systems.authentication_notes: Uses HTTP Basic Authentication. The 'username' is your Auth ID and the 'password' is your Auth Token. Both are found in the Plivo console and must be included in the 'Authorization' header.

telephony_systems.name: Vonage (formerly Nexmo)
telephony_systems.authentication_notes: Supports two methods: 1) Basic Authentication using 'api_key' and 'api_secret' as query parameters or in the header. 2) JWT (JSON Web Token) for newer APIs, requiring an Application ID and a Private Key.

telephony_systems.name: SignalWire
telephony_systems.authentication_notes: Uses HTTP Basic Authentication. The 'username' is your Project ID and the 'password' is your API Token. Requires your Space URL (e.g., your-space.signalwire.com) as the base for all API calls.

telephony_systems.name: Telnyx
telephony_systems.authentication_notes: Uses Bearer Token authentication. You must include your API Key in the 'Authorization' header as 'Bearer {YOUR_API_KEY}'. API keys are managed in the Telnyx Portal under the 'API Keys' section.

telephony_systems.name: MessageBird
telephony_systems.authentication_notes: Uses a custom Authorization header format: 'Authorization: AccessKey {YOUR_ACCESS_KEY}'. Access keys can be 'live' or 'test' (prefixed with test_) and are managed in the MessageBird Dashboard.

effort_recommendation.rationale: No native node - requires HTTP Request configuration
